subject: Science
question_text: Which one of the following food item does not provide dietary fibre?
answer_key: Milk
answer_explanation: Milk is an animal product and does not contain dietary fibre (roughage), which is exclusively provided by plant products.
provenance_excerpt: Which one of the following food item does not provide dietary fibre?

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following sources of protein is different from others?
answer_key: Cottage cheese (paneer)
answer_explanation: Cottage cheese is an animal-based protein source (from milk), while peas, gram, and soyabeans are plant-based protein sources.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following sources of protein is different from others?

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following nutrients is not present in milk?
answer_key: Vitamin C
answer_explanation: Milk contains protein, calcium, and vitamin D, but it lacks vitamin C, which is typically found in citrus fruits.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following nutrients is not present in milk?

subject: Science
question_text:
Read the food items given below:
(i) Wheat
(ii) Ghee
(iii) Iodised salt
(iv) Spinach (palak)
Which of the above food items are 'energy giving foods'?
answer_key: (i) and (ii)
answer_explanation: Wheat is rich in carbohydrates and ghee is rich in fats. Both carbohydrates and fats are energy-giving foods.

subject: Science
question_text:
Read the following statements about diseases.
(i) They are caused by germs.
(ii) They are caused due to lack of nutrients in our diet.
(iii) They can be passed on to another person through contact.
(iv) They can be prevented by taking a balanced diet.
Which pair of statements best describe a deficiency disease?
answer_key: (ii) and (iv)
answer_explanation: Deficiency diseases are caused by a lack of nutrients (not germs) and can be prevented by a balanced diet.

(i) They are caused by germs.

subject: Science
question_text:
Given below are the steps to test the presence of proteins in a food item:
(i) Take a small quantity of the food item in a test tube, add 10 drops of water to it and shake it.
(ii) Make a paste or powder of food to be tested.
(iii) Add 10 drops of caustic soda solution to the test tube and shake well.
(iv) Add 2 drops of copper sulphate solution to it.
Which of the following is the correct sequence of the steps?
answer_key: ii, i, iv, iii
answer_explanation: First make a paste (ii), take in a test tube with water (i), add 2 drops of copper sulphate (iv), and then 10 drops of caustic soda (iii).

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following food items does not provide any nutrient?
answer_key: Dietary fibres (roughage) and water do not provide any nutrient to our body.
answer_explanation: Although water and roughage are essential for our body, they do not provide nutrients.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following food items does not provide any nutrient?

subject: Science
question_text:
Fill in the blanks from the list of words given below: (carbohydrate, fat, protein, starch, sugar, Vitamin A, Vitamin C, roughage, balanced diet, obesity, goitre)
(a) Egg yolk is rich in ________ and egg albumin is rich in ________.
(b) Deficiency diseases can be prevented by taking a ________.
(c) Eating too much of fat rich foods may lead to a condition called ________.
(d) The component of food that does not provide any nutrient to our body and yet is essential in our food is ________.
(e) The vitamin that gets easily destroyed by heating during cooking is ________.
answer_key:
(a) fat, protein
(b) balanced diet
(c) obesity
(d) roughage
(e) Vitamin C
answer_explanation: Key concepts around diet and nutrients.

subject: Science
question_text:
Read the items of food listed below. Classify them into carbohydrate rich, protein rich and fat rich foods:
Moong dal, fish, mustard oil, sweet potato, milk, rice, egg, beans, butter, butter milk (chhachh), cottage cheese (paneer), peas, maize, white bread.
answer_key:
Carbohydrate rich: sweet potato, rice, maize, white bread.
Protein rich: moong dal, fish, milk, egg, beans, butter milk, cottage cheese, peas.
Fat rich: mustard oil, butter, milk, egg.
answer_explanation: Classification based on major nutrient components. Some items like milk and egg contain significant amounts of both protein and fat.

subject: Science
question_text: Tasty food is not always nutritious and nutritious food may not always be tasty to eat. Comment with examples.
answer_key: Food items like potato chips, burgers, and pizzas are very tasty but they contain a lot of fat and refined carbohydrates, making them less nutritious. Conversely, boiled green vegetables or bitter gourd (karela) are highly nutritious but may not taste very good to everyone.
answer_explanation: Taste vs. Nutritional Value.
provenance_excerpt: Tasty food is not always nutritious and nutritious food may not always be tasty to eat. Comment with examples.

subject: Science
question_text: While using iodine in the laboratory, some drops of iodine fell on Paheli’s socks and some fell on her teacher’s saree. The drops of iodine on the saree turned blue black while their colour did not change on the socks. What can be the possible reason?
answer_key: The teacher's saree might have been starched. The iodine changed its colour to blue-black upon reacting with the starch. Since the socks did not have starch, no colour change was observed.
answer_explanation: Iodine turns blue-black in the presence of starch.
provenance_excerpt: While using iodine in the laboratory, some drops of iodine fell on Paheli’s socks and some fell on her teacher’s saree.

subject: Science
question_text: Paheli and Boojho peeled some potatoes and cut them into small pieces. They washed and boiled them in water. They threw away the excess water and fried them in oil adding salt and spices. Although the potato dish tasted very good, its nutrient value was less. Suggest a method of cooking potatoes that will not lower the nutrients in them.
answer_key: To retain the nutrients: Wash the potatoes before peeling or cutting. Boil them in just enough water so it doesn't need to be thrown away, or boil them with their skin on. Avoid deep frying.
answer_explanation: Cooking methods can lead to the loss of water-soluble vitamins and minerals.

subject: Science
question_text: Paheli avoids eating vegetables but likes to eat biscuits, noodles and white bread. She frequently complains of stomachache and constipation. What are the food items that she should include in her diet to get rid of the problem? Give reason for your answer.
answer_key: She should include roughage (dietary fibre) in her diet by eating fresh fruits, green leafy vegetables, and whole grains. Roughage adds bulk to the food and helps in the easy movement of bowel, preventing constipation.
answer_explanation: Role of dietary fibre in digestion.

subject: Science
question_text:
(a) List all those components of food that provide nutrients.
(b) Mention two components of food that do not provide nutrients.
answer_key:
(a) Carbohydrates, fats, proteins, vitamins, and minerals.
(b) Water and dietary fibres (roughage).
answer_explanation: Differentiation between nutrients and non-nutrient essential components.

subject: Science
question_text: 'Minerals and vitamins are needed in very small quantities by our body as compared to other components, yet, they are an important part of a balanced diet.' Explain the statement.
answer_key: Though needed in small amounts, they are essential for protecting the body against diseases, maintaining good health, normal growth, and proper functioning of the body. Their lack causes deficiency diseases.
answer_explanation: Importance of micronutrients.

subject: Science
question_text: 'Water does not provide nutrients, yet it is an important component of food.' Explain?
answer_key: Water helps our body absorb nutrients from food and it is essential for throwing out some wastes from the body in the form of urine and sweat.
answer_explanation: Functions of water in the body.
provenance_excerpt: 'Water does not provide nutrients, yet it is an important component of food.' Explain?

subject: Science
question_text:
Boojho was having difficulty in seeing things in dim light. The doctor tested his eyesight and prescribed a particular vitamin supplement. He also advised him to include a few food items in his diet.
(a) Which deficiency disease is he suffering from?
(b) Which food component may be lacking in his diet?
(c) Suggest some food items that he should include in his diet. (any four)
answer_key:
(a) Night blindness.
(b) Vitamin A.
(c) Carrot, papaya, mango, milk, spinach, fish liver oil.
answer_explanation: Understanding of Vitamin A deficiency.

question_text:

(d) Milk provides __________, vitamin D and __________ (mineral)

(b) phosphorus/calcium, vitamin D
(c) Butter/Oil
(d) protein, calcium
(e) Papaya/Mango
(f) iron
(g) meat/fish, protein
